signals.reasoning: Cover the live LIT short immediately. The trade has gone from about +25 dollars peak unrealized to roughly -26 dollars live, retained none of peak PnL, is flagged urgent, and still has no stop. Even if the broader chart still looks weak, this specific position has already failed decisively and should be exited as risk cleanup.

BTC is still fragile under the active geopolitical veto, but the immediate tape is a post-flush stabilization rather than a clean new trade. After exiting LIT, patience is better than forcing a second position here.
ETH remains soft but has already absorbed a large liquidation move. It is not a higher-quality action point than simply flattening the broken LIT position.
SOL is weak but now trading more like a stabilization zone after the selloff. Keep it on the bearish watchlist without forcing action under veto.

IP has squeeze fuel from negative funding and heavy OI crowding, but the macro event brake is active and the chart is still messy. It is a watchlist name, not a better immediate trade than cleaning up LIT.
BLAST remains bearish on paper, but it is thin and already in liquidation-flush territory. That makes it unsuitable as a fresh follow-on trade.
GOAT has extreme negative funding and a severe washout profile, which raises squeeze risk more than it creates a trustworthy short. Too unstable to act on here.
FARTCOIN is still weak, but the move already looks like a completed long-liquidation flush rather than an early continuation entry. Watch only.
MERL is still soft after the broader selloff, but the setup is already extended and too low-conviction to justify acting while veto is active.
FTT is too thin and too structurally noisy to trust under current conditions. Not a serious candidate for action.
DASH is still damaged after the flush, but that also means the easy part of the short has already happened. Better as a watchlist name than a new position.
